# OpenClaw Gateway Dashboard Fork And Chat Condition Work Order
GPAO-T must be built by absorbing the user's live OpenClaw, not by drifting into a separate decorative surface.

The OpenClaw Gateway Dashboard is the current visible body. It already provides a clean local control shell, session navigation, chat composer, model/status controls, settings, skills, nodes, documents, and local runtime state.

The goal is not to improve OpenClaw for OpenClaw's sake. The goal is to turn this dashboard body into a GPAO-T Control Center: a Codex-like multi-conversation personal AI work OS powered by GPAO Core, T-cell admission, Context Mesh, authori
1. OpenClaw Gateway Dashboard in Safari on `127.0.0.1`
- Left rail: OpenClaw Control branding, `New session`, `Main Session`, usage, recent/chat/control/agent/settings/document sections.
- Shows that OpenClaw/Telegram can display live work progress while the task is running.

- nbeai GitHub repositories are the first source library for GPAO / BEAI Package / OpenClaw-facing package / Codex-facing package / Knowledge Loop / T-cell-related prior work. Hardware Engine material is retired from the current GPAO-T scop
- Dashboard work must avoid final sidecar/plugin-only integration. The dashboard is part of the OpenClaw body that must be transformed from within.
The target is a Codex-like multi-conversation work OS built out of OpenClaw's dashboard body.
- OpenClaw's local runtime stability is still underneath, but the visible experience is GPAO-T.
## 4. What To Keep From OpenClaw Dashboard
Before mutating live OpenClaw, use pure source anatomy and local fork/lab work.
- No live OpenClaw mutation unless explicitly approved.
- Mutating live OpenClaw files outside the workspace boundary.

- Remaining `beai-harness-for-codex` full-test failures are separate from this missing-module fix. They are tied to pre-existing deleted OpenClaw adapter paths and some blocked/review expectation drift.
- The user can recognize the OpenClaw Gateway Dashboard body.
- OpenClaw remains the material, not the product owner.
- 팀원 alpha handoff에 포함된 Codex / OpenClaw / Claude Code host integration matrix가 ready여야 한다.
- Codex / OpenClaw / Claude Code별 local stdio MCP 등록 모드
- Codex / OpenClaw / Claude Code host setup evidence가 포함되어야 한다.
MCP host smoke로 Codex/OpenClaw/Claude Code 공통 호출 가능성을 확인한다.
이 문서는 `사장님 자동화 도우미`를 팀원 alpha에서 Codex, OpenClaw, Claude Code 같은 호스트에 로컬 stdio MCP로 붙여 보는 안내서다.
OpenClaw MCP/skill host 설정에서 같은 stdio 명령을 연결한다. OpenClaw Gateway에 공개 route를 추가하는 단계가 아니다.
`host-integration-matrix`는 Codex, OpenClaw, Claude Code에서 같은 Owner Ops MCP 서버를 어떻게 등록하고 검증할지 한 화면의 계약으로 보여준다.

completeSeal.completionRule: status is ready only when no forbidden OpenClaw-derived product identity appears in user-visible surfaces.

dashboardRoutes.nextVerification: Route seal automation is connected to the current Safari DOM readback evidence. Re-run browser readback after each visible dashboard patch.

runtimeNamespace.migrationRule: Do not blind-replace runtime namespace keys. Add GPAO-T mirrors, preserve existing sessions, then cut over with rollback evidence.

livePatches.completionRule: ready requires every live mutation family to have a source tool, token/dry-run gate, backup or rollback evidence, structured manifest or receipt, and readback command.

sourceGroups.completionRule: ready means every current git status entry is assigned to a review lane. It does not stage or commit files.

packageIntegrity.completionRule: ready requires archive, sha256 receipt, manifest, and bundle files to exist and the archive hash to match its receipt.

completionRule: This seal can close supervised local test-team handoff only when user-visible identity, routes, live patch reproducibility, source evidence ownership, package integrity, and runtime namespace gates pass. Clean-machine install, signed/public distribution, and source-level independent package proof remain separate release lanes.
